benefits: Stretches the shoulders, hamstrings, calves, and hands.
Strengthens the arms and legs.
Calms the brain and helps relieve stress and mild depression.
Energizes the body by bringing fresh blood to the head.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Can be performed at any time; often used as a transitional or resting pose.

contraindications: Recent or chronic injury to the wrists, shoulders, or back.
High blood pressure or headache (must modify: use a block for head support).

benefits: Improves balance, concentration, and focus.
Strengthens the thighs, calves, ankles, and spine.
Stretches the groins and inner thigh.
Helps relieve sciatica and reduce flat feet.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds to 1 minute per side (5 to 10 breaths)
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Great for morning practice to establish focus and grounding.

suitable_age_group: 8 to 70 years (can use a wall for support)
contraindications: High blood pressure (do not raise hands above the heart).

benefits: Strengthens the spine and deep back muscles.
Stretches the chest, shoulders, and abdomen.
Tones the abdominal organs.
Improves spinal flexibility and posture.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 15 to 30 seconds (3 to 5 breaths), repeated 3-5 times.
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Excellent as part of a Sun Salutation or to counter forward-bending poses.

contraindications: Recent abdominal surgery or severe abdominal injury (e.g., hernia).
Severe back injury (e.g., slipped disc or acute sciatica flare-up).

benefits: Gently stretches the hips, thighs, and ankles.
Calms the brain and helps relieve stress and fatigue.
Relieves back and neck pain when supported.
Encourages deep, slow breathing.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds up to 5 minutes or longer as a resting pose.
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Used for rest between challenging poses, or as a concluding, calming pose.

contraindications: Knee injury (must use a thick blanket or cushion between calves and thighs).
Pregnancy (widen knees substantially to accommodate the abdomen).

benefits: Strengthens and stretches the legs, ankles, and chest.
Increases stamina and concentration.
Stimulates abdominal organs.
Relieves backache, especially during the second trimester of pregnancy.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds to 1 minute per side (5 to 10 breaths)
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Part of a flow or standing sequence, excellent for building heat and focus.

contraindications: Recent or chronic knee or hip injury (modify by using props or reducing the depth of the lunge).

benefits: Firms the abdomen and buttocks.
Improves posture and corrects flat feet.
Strengthens the thighs, knees, and ankles.
Increases consciousness and presence.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Starting and ending pose for standing sequences; can be done anytime for posture correction.

benefits: Strengthens the core, arms, wrists, and spine.
Tones the abdominal organs.
Builds stamina and mental focus.
Prepares the body for arm balances and inversions.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Often a transition in Vinyasa flow, or practiced independently for core strength.

benefits: Stretches the hips, groins, hamstrings, and calves.
Strengthens the legs, knees, and ankles.
Relieves backache and mild stress.
Aids in digestion.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds to 1 minute per side (5-8 breaths).
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Best in a standing sequence when the body is warm.

benefits: Calms the brain and helps relieve stress.
Stretches the spine, hamstrings, and shoulders.
Stimulates the liver, kidneys, ovaries, and uterus.
Aids digestion and relieves menstrual discomfort.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Ideal towards the end of a practice; best performed on an empty stomach.

suitable_age_group: 10 to 80+ years (with bent knees for stiff backs)
contraindications: Back injury (only practice with bent knees and a minimal fold).
Pregnancy (only a very minimal fold with widely spread legs).

benefits: Stretches the chest, neck, and spine.
Calms the brain and alleviates stress and mild depression.
Stimulates abdominal organs, thyroid, and lungs.
Helps relieve symptoms of menopause and menstrual discomfort.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds to 1 minute (5-10 breaths), repeated 3-5 times.
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Can be used as a therapeutic pose or a mild backbend preparation.

contraindications: Neck injury (must practice with extreme caution and supervision).

benefits: Strengthens the abdomen, spine, and hip flexors.
Stimulates the kidneys, thyroid, and prostate glands.
Aids in digestion and relieves stress.
Improves balance and focus.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 15 seconds to 30 seconds (3-5 breaths), repeated 3 times.
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Best in the middle of a practice to build heat and core strength.

ailments_helped: Low back pain (strengthening the core helps stability)
contraindications: Recent abdominal surgery or lower back injury.

english_name: Half Lord of the Fishes Pose
benefits: Stretches the shoulders, hips, and neck.
Energizes the spine and stimulates the abdominal organs (liver and kidneys).
Relieves backache, fatigue, and sciatica.
Opens the chest and improves breathing.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds to 1 minute per side (5-8 breaths).
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Excellent for improving spinal mobility and digestion, often practiced before savasana.

suitable_age_group: 10 to 80+ years (modify twist depth for seniors)
contraindications: Severe back injury or spinal disc issues (twists should be gentle).
Pregnancy (only a very mild open twist).

benefits: Strengthens the thighs, calves, ankles, and spine.
Tones the shoulders, abdomen, and hip flexors.
Stimulates the heart, diaphragm, and abdominal organs.
Relieves flat feet.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: High-energy pose, best performed in the middle of a practice to build heat.

benefits: Strengthens the legs, knees, and ankles.
Stretches the groins, spine, waist, chest, and shoulders.
Stimulates abdominal organs and increases stamina.
Helps relieve sciatica and menstrual discomfort.

timing_guidance.hold_duration: 30 seconds to 1 minute per side (5-10 breaths).
timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Part of a standing pose sequence after warming up.

contraindications: Neck injury (keep the head in line with the spine).

benefits: Strengthens the arms, wrists, abdominal muscles, and chest.
Improves endurance and core stability.
Prepares the body for more advanced arm balances.
Tones the entire body.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Used as a transition in Vinyasa style classes; requires significant warm-up.

contraindications: Low back injury (must modify with knees down).

benefits: Stretches the hips, groins, and ankles.
Tones the abdomen and improves digestion.
Increases circulation and blood flow to the pelvis.
Relieves back pain in the lower spine.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Excellent for opening the hips, can be done anytime.

suitable_age_group: 10 to 80+ years (can sit on a block for support)

benefits: Calms the brain and central nervous system.
Stretches the shoulders and spine.
Stimulates the thyroid gland and abdominal organs.
Helps relieve stress, fatigue, and insomnia.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Best performed late in practice, often following Shoulderstand.

contraindications: Asthma, high blood pressure, or glaucoma.

benefits: Relaxes the whole body, reducing stress and calming the brain.
Reduces headache, fatigue, and insomnia.
Lowers blood pressure.
Allows the body to absorb the full benefits of the practice.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: ALWAYS the final pose of any yoga session.

contraindications: None, unless lying flat on the back is painful (use blankets/bolsters for support).

benefits: Calms the brain and relieves mild depression.
Stretches the spine, hamstrings, and groins.
Stimulates the liver and kidneys.
Relieves anxiety, fatigue, and menstrual discomfort.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: Resting forward fold, best practiced after seated twists or standing sequences.

suitable_age_group: 10 to 80+ years (can use a strap for the foot)
contraindications: Knee injury (be extremely careful with the bent knee).
Back injury (must sit on a blanket and avoid excessive folding).

benefits: Strengthens the arms, wrists, and abdominal organs.
Stretches the upper back and improves balance.
Tones the core and strengthens mental focus.
Aids in preparing for headstand and other inversions.

timing_guidance.when_to_perform: High-energy pose, best done in the middle of a practice when the body is fully warm.

contraindications: Carpal tunnel syndrome or wrist injury.
